name: Vesta
summary: Vesta is one of the largest bodies in the asteroid belt, with a differentiated interior and basaltic surface. Studied by NASA's Dawn mission.

name: Pallas
summary: Pallas is a large, inclined asteroid in the main belt and was the second asteroid discovered. It has a heavily cratered, primitive surface.

name: Hygiea
summary: Hygiea is one of the largest asteroids and a dark, carbonaceous body; recent work suggests it may be nearly spherical.

name: 433 Eros
summary: 433 Eros is a near-Earth asteroid visited by the NEAR Shoemaker spacecraft; its surface shows boulders, regolith and a complex cratered history.

name: 101955 Bennu
summary: Bennu is a near-Earth carbonaceous asteroid studied by NASA's OSIRIS-REx mission; samples have been returned to Earth for laboratory analysis.

name: 25143 Itokawa
summary: Itokawa is a small, rubble-pile near-Earth asteroid visited by JAXA's Hayabusa mission; it is irregular in shape and composed of loose aggregates.

name: Ceres
summary: Ceres is both the largest asteroid and a dwarf planet in the main belt. (Also listed in dwarf-planets.json for convenience.)
